mission.title: jaeger: jaeger-agent reproducible memory leak in 1.21.0
mission.description: jaeger-agent reproducible memory leak in 1.21.0. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify jaeger troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your jaeger deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger -n observability --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `don't know what type: 15","stacktrace":"github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors.(*ThriftProcessor).proc`

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
I am observing very high and rapidly increasing memory usage of `jaeger-agent` which may be a memory leak.
Eventually the agent (container) may run out of memory and crash.

**I am able to reproduce the behavior reliably.

steps.title: Apply the fix for jaeger-agent reproducible memory leak in 1.21.0
steps.description:
Status update: I got a reproducer that touches far less code, so, might be easier to isolate and fix the issue:

```go
package main

import
```yaml
jaeger-agent        | {"level":"error","ts":1605909716.7062023,"caller":"processors/thrift_processor.go:123","msg":"Processor failed","error":"*jaeger.Batch error reading struct: *jaeger.Span error reading struct: error reading list begin: don't know what type: 14","stacktrace":"github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors.(*ThriftProcessor).processBuffer\n\tgithub.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors/thrift_processor.go:123\ngithub.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors.NewThriftProcessor.func2\n\tgithub.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors/thr
```

steps.title: Confirm jaeger-agent reproducible memory leak in 1.21.0 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger -n observability --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `don't know what type: 15","stacktrace":"github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/cmd/agent/app/processors.(*ThriftProcessor).proc` no longer appears in logs.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
